title: A show of strength from Ukrainian society
subhed: The spontaneous mobilization of Ukrainians, who took to the streets to voice their anger over the dismissal of their defense minister despite relentless Russian strikes, paid off. The country's cohesion ultimately prevailed, while a prolonged crisis would have weakened Ukraine's standing with its allies.

title: Tariffs: Trump pursues his trade war through every method available
After two diplomats were violently detained in Tehran, France expresses concern for its nationals in Iran
US unveils new tariffs on 60 partners as Trump rebuilds trade agenda
subhed: The levies, effective Friday, range from 10% to 12.5% and impact major economies like China, India and the EU. Earlier this week, the US president also ordered new 50% tariffs on many Canadian products, citing Ottawa's 'discriminatory treatment' of American products including alcohol and automobiles.

title: My life as a Russian exile in Paris: 'France is an improved version of the USSR'
subhed: 'A Russian in Paris' (2/6). After arriving in France in 2022, journalist Elizaveta Osetinskaya settled in Paris' 16th arrondissement. She especially praises the French school system, which her daughter Bella now benefits from.

title: Bab Al-Mandab Strait threatens to be new front in war with Iran
subhed: The Houthi forces, who announced a blockade of Saudi ports on Monday, claimed responsibility for an attack in the Red Sea targeting two Saudi Arabian oil tankers on Wednesday. Iran and the Houthis are betting that the economic consequences of the war will bring Donald Trump back to the negotiating table.

title: Wildfires in southern France: 'The anxiety comes afterward, when we see the scorched landscape'
subhed: Fires are raging in southwestern France, including a blaze that began on Wednesday, which forced the precautionary evacuation of 20,000 people, destroyed six houses and devastated everything along a stretch of some 10 kilometers.

subhed: Monique Barbut, who said she intended to quit the government on Tuesday, July 21, after an agricultural law she contested was adopted, ended up staying on in her role on Wednesday. The twist added a colorful note to the political crisis.

title: Salah Abdeslam, punished for protesting his prison conditions, challenges the administration
subhed: The last surviving member of the group behind the November 13, 2015, Paris terrorist attacks, Salah Abdeslam was sentenced to life without parole and solitary confinement for 10 years. He has now chosen to confront the authorities.

title: My life as a Russian exile in Paris: 'You can't fight French bureaucracy'
subhed: 'A Russian in Paris' (1/6). Journalist Elizaveta Osetinskaya, who came to France in 2022 because of her opposition to the war in Ukraine, describes the difficulties she has faced with the prefecture while barely speaking the language.
